text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button
rendered with no icon and no text. Pass it for both args.

commit.message:
Comprehensive security, stability, and quality improvements across all 4 phases

Phase 1 — Critical Security & Stability:
- Deploy lock via flock to prevent concurrent deployments
- Cleanup trap in deploy.sh to remove half-finished releases on failure
- grep -F for credential lookups to prevent regex injection
- mysql_safe() helper to avoid password exposure in process list
- Rollback cache commands are now non-fatal

Phase 2 — Security & Deployment Quality:
- NodeSource install via APT repo instead of curl|bash
- sed_escape_value() for safe .env and config handling
- Template engine escapes pipe delimiter, writes atomically, warns on unreplaced placeholders
- Disk space check (500MB min) before deployment
- Health check after deployment
- Pre-migration database backup
- Timeouts and retry logic for network operations

Phase 3 — Scaling & Observability:
- FPM pool sizing is now multi-site aware (dynamic RAM allocation)
- MariaDB buffer pool reduced from 70% to 40% RAM
- Supervisor stopwaitsecs reduced from 3600/600 to 30
- Logrotate uses copytruncate instead of supervisorctl restart all
- Rate limiting applied in nginx vhost PHP location blocks
- Scheduler cron logs to file instead of /dev/null
- New forge-lite status command
- Optional test step in CI/CD workflow

Phase 4 — Maintainability:
- ShellCheck + bash -n CI pipeline (.github/workflows/lint.yml)
- VERSION file + forge-lite --version flag
- CLI tools source lib/common.sh with fallback (eliminates log duplication)
- add-site.sh cleanup trap on partial failure
- TOCTOU fix in credentials.sh (install -m 600 instead of touch+chmod)
- Removed invalid local keywords from top-level scope in remove-site.sh
- CI/CD failure notification step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix webhook deployment: wait for cert before checking readiness

The controller-manager pod won't start until the webhook-server-cert
secret exists. Wait for the cert-manager Certificate to be Ready
before checking deployment readiness. Also mark the cert volume as
optional so the pod can be scheduled while cert-manager provisions
the certificate.

Webhook noise reduction, occupancy support, and README improvements (#24)

  instead of "Input Event", "Door Open/Closed" instead of

- Filter command/target characteristics from webhook trigger list

Battery characteristics (battery_level, low_battery) fire every 30-60s

that bury actual deliveries. Silently drop them instead.

full-text search with better maturity and Block-Max WAND optimization.

- VectorChord 1.0.0 -> 1.1.1
- pg_textsearch 0.6.0 (new, replaces VectorChord-bm25 0.2.2 + pg_tokenizer 0.1.1)
- Base image postgres:18.1 -> 18.3

feat: add GHA pipeline workflow and gha-dispatch/run-issue CLI commands (#13)

* feat: add GHA pipeline workflow and run-issue CLI command (closes #5)

- Add .github/workflows/pipeline.yml that triggers breadforge dispatch
  on issues labeled stage/impl, passing the issue number and repo to
  the new run-issue command
- Add run-issue command to cli.py that fetches issue metadata, seeds a
  WorkBead, locates a matching spec by milestone title, and dispatches
  via the graph executor (falling back to rolling dispatcher when no
  spec is found)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add gha-dispatch CLI command and pipeline workflow (closes #5)

- Add `.github/workflows/pipeline.yml`: triggers on `issues.labeled`
  events when the `stage/impl` label is added to an issue with a
  milestone; installs uv + breadforge then runs `breadforge gha-dispatch`
- Add `breadforge gha-dispatch` CLI command: reads GITHUB_EVENT_NAME,
  GITHUB_EVENT_PATH, and GITHUB_REPOSITORY from the GHA environment,
  finds the matching spec file under specs/ for the issue's milestone,
  and runs the graph executor end-to-end

commit.message:
v1.3: Deal history context injection (Iter 3)

Adds a persistent deal log that feeds directly into intel generation:

State & persistence:
- dealHistory: localStorage key prospect_deal_history
- dealForm: company name, size, deal size, outcome, angle/reason, notes
- Each deal tagged to a NAICS code + sector

Prompt injection:
- Before Phase 2 generation, finds relevant deals (same NAICS code
  OR same sector) and formats them as WON/LOST deal blocks
- Instructs model to weight angles toward what converted and flag
  loss patterns as additional red flags

UI (below input panel):
- "Deal History" card always visible
- "+ Log a deal" toggle opens inline form
- Won/lost outcome toggle with conditional angle or lost reason field
- Deal entries show company name, size, deal size, angle/reason, notes
- ✕ button to remove individual entries
- Won entries in green, lost in red
- Relevant deal count badge when deals exist for the vertical

feat: add credential proxy module with scoped token auth (closes #4) (#14)

- proxy/token.py: HMAC-SHA256 signed scoped tokens (anthropic/openai/google)
  with configurable expiry; validates scope, signature, and expiry
- proxy/server.py: loopback HTTP proxy (127.0.0.1, random port) that
  validates scoped tokens, injects real credentials, and streams responses
- proxy/__init__.py: package exports (CredentialProxy, issue_token, validate_token)
- agents/runner.py: _build_env and run_agent accept proxy_url/proxy_token;
  when proxy is active real API keys are withheld from subprocesses
- health.py: warn when BREADFORGE_PROXY_SECRET is unset (ephemeral key used)

The Supabase Management API returns 429 when hit too frequently.

increase test timeout to 30s to handle retries in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix DB test rate limiting: add retry logic to all HTTP calls

Wrap GoTrue Admin API fetch calls with exponential backoff retry
for 429 responses. Increase max retries to 5 with 3s base delay
and random jitter to handle CI rate limits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix DB test rate limiting with throttling and file-based user cache

- Add 300ms throttle between API calls to prevent request bursts
- Cache resolved test user to file so subsequent test files skip
  DB lookup entirely (Jest resets module state between files)
- Add throttle file and user cache to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix claim-milestone test: use throttled runSQL, increase timeout

- Remove raw runSQL import that bypassed throttle/retry
- Add cleanupMilestones helper using throttled wrapper
- Increase test timeout to 60s (throttle + retries need headroom)
- Add forceExit to prevent Jest hanging after tests complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce DB test API calls: batch cleanup, remove broken throttle

- Remove compounding throttle (wrote future timestamps, causing
  snowballing delays that exceeded test timeouts)
- Add cleanupAndReset helper that batches DELETE + point reset
  into a single API call (saves ~30 calls across all suites)
- Replace all raw runSQL imports with retry-wrapped runSQLWithRetry
- Increase test timeout to 60s for retry headroom

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce retry delays and add jest.retryTimes for rate limiting

- Reduce MAX_RETRIES to 3 with 1.5s base delay (max 10.5s per
  call vs 93s before) so retries fit within 60s test timeout
- Add jest.retryTimes(1) so rate-limited tests get a second chance
  after the rate limit window passes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix jest config: remove invalid setupFilesAfterFramework, add retryTimes

- Remove setupFilesAfterFramework (not a valid Jest option)
- Delete db-setup-after-env.ts
- Add jest.retryTimes(1) directly in each test file instead
- Reduced retry delays (3 retries, 1.5s base) already committed

commit.message:
Refactor pipeline to stream skills one at a time (#5)

## Summary
- Process each skill through the full pipeline (download → transform →
publish) before moving to the next, instead of batching each phase
- Reduces peak memory usage from holding all skills simultaneously to
one at a time
- Saves state after each successful publish for crash resilience
(previously only saved once at the end)
- Adds `[1/50]` progress logging per skill

## Test plan
- [x] All 48 existing tests pass
- [ ] Run `clawhub-importer --dry-run` to verify streaming behavior
- [ ] Run `clawhub-importer --publish --slugs <slug>` to verify
single-skill publish + state save

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: elias-memory v0.1.0 — persistent memory framework for AI agents

- Exponential decay (7-day half-life, 0.01 floor)

- HuggingFace SFT JSONL export
- 38 tests, all passing

Multi-model optimized design (Qwen-Coder, Step-Flash, Kimi-K2, Nemotron).

Add live dynamic worker registry and content sync layer

and parsePriceTier(). Extends workerSync.js with syncApprovedWorker(),
deprecateWorker(), rebuildAllCounters(), and content sync triggers.

workers, Alex dynamic context builder, and WorkerCounter frontend
component with real-time onSnapshot listener. P0.18 enforced: no worker
goes live without passing Worker #1 pipeline.

commit.message:
fix(helm): rename helpers to avoid parent chart collision (#49)

Renames all helper templates from kagent.* to kagent-tools.* prefix to
prevent naming conflicts with the parent kagent chart. When Helm renders
subcharts, template definitions are global, causing the parent chart's
helpers to override the subchart's helpers with the same names.

This fixes:
- Selector label mismatch when using nameOverride (was using parent's
  logic instead of subchart's fullname logic)
- Helm upgrade failures due to immutable selector field changes
- Enables proper use of nameOverride instead of requiring
  fullnameOverride workaround

All helper references updated across all template files:
- _helpers.tpl: Renamed 10 helper definitions
- deployment.yaml, service.yaml, serviceaccount.yaml: Updated references
- clusterrole.yaml, clusterrolebinding.yaml: Updated references
- servicemonitor.yaml, NOTES.txt: Updated references

Backward compatible: existing fullnameOverride usage continues to work.

Signed-off-by: Eitan Yarmush <eitan.yarmush@solo.io>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: inverted controls no longer cause jump on return-to-dot dismiss

When dismissing return-to-dot with inverted debuff active, the raw
mouse position was set as targetX/Y, then the update loop inverted it,
causing the player to jump to the opposite side. Now pre-inverts the
target so it cancels out correctly.

commit.message:
Claude/ski tracker 8o i9v (#7)

* Track and display exact lift opening times

- Add first_opened_at column to daily_summary table with migration for existing DBs
- Record timestamp when a lift is first seen as "open" each day
- Display opening time in status pill (e.g. "Opened 9:30 AM") instead of just "Opened"
- Backfill existing opening times from terrain_snapshots on startup
- Add /api/last-scrape endpoint to diagnose missing scrape data
- Pass scraped_at through all update_daily_summary call sites

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix resort cards not showing and add snapshot backfill for missing dates

Root cause: /api/status only returned resorts with daily_summary data.
When the scraper failed, no terrain rows existed, so ALL resort cards
disappeared — including weather that was working independently.

Fixes:
- Always return all 4 tracked resorts in /api/status even without terrain data
- Add try/except to run_scrape() with per-resort error logging
- Add backfill_daily_from_snapshots() to recover daily_summary from
  terrain_snapshots when the save pipeline crashed partway through
- Auto-backfill last 7 days of snapshot data on startup
- Add POST /api/backfill endpoint for manual date recovery

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Add Powder Mountain (James Peak), 6am scraper start, smart digest triggering

- Add Powder Mountain scraper tracking James Peak terrain via Playwright
- Change terrain scrape schedule from 7am to 6am MT start
- Replace fixed digest cron with smart triggering: auto-generates after
  both avalanche forecast and terrain data are available for the day
- Keep 10am fallback digest in case auto-trigger doesn't fire
- Add Powder Mountain to weather forecasts, digest, and frontend
- Keep avalanche schedule at 5-9am as requested

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Make daily digest collapsible — starts minimized, click to expand

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Update README for current deployed state: 5 resorts, Fly.io, API docs

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix deploy path in README: ~/ski-tracker not ~/ski-tracker-deploy

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Revert deploy path back to ~/ski-tracker-deploy

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Update README deploy path to ~/ski-tracker

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Add avalanche danger rose SVG and make section collapsible

- Render UAC-style danger rose from existing danger_rose data (24 values: 8 aspects x 3 elevations)
- Color-coded wedges: green (low), yellow (moderate), orange (considerable), red (high)
- Collapsible section matching digest pattern (click header to expand/collapse)
- Flex layout with rose alongside summary text, stacks on mobile

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Include avalanche fetch in manual scrape + skip if already fetched

- /api/scrape now also calls fetch_avalanche_forecast() and
  maybe_generate_digest() so the danger rose populates on manual trigger
- Scheduled run_avalanche() skips fetch if today's forecast already
  exists in the DB, avoiding unnecessary UAC API hits

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix circular import: remove start import from app.py

The previous commit imported maybe_generate_digest from start.py
into app.py, but start.py already imports from app.py — circular
import. Removed it; digest generation still happens via the
scheduled fallback.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Make daily digest collapsible (#5)

* Track and display exact lift opening times

- Add first_opened_at column to daily_summary table with migration for existing DBs
- Record timestamp when a lift is first seen as "open" each day
- Display opening time in status pill (e.g. "Opened 9:30 AM") instead of just "Opened"
- Backfill existing opening times from terrain_snapshots on startup
- Add /api/last-scrape endpoint to diagnose missing scrape data
- Pass scraped_at through all update_daily_summary call sites

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix resort cards not showing and add snapshot backfill for missing dates

Root cause: /api/status only returned resorts with daily_summary data.
When the scraper failed, no terrain rows existed, so ALL resort cards
disappeared — including weather that was working independently.

Fixes:
- Always return all 4 tracked resorts in /api/status even without terrain data
- Add try/except to run_scrape() with per-resort error logging
- Add backfill_daily_from_snapshots() to recover daily_summary from
  terrain_snapshots when the save pipeline crashed partway through
- Auto-backfill last 7 days of snapshot data on startup
- Add POST /api/backfill endpoint for manual date recovery

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Add Powder Mountain (James Peak), 6am scraper start, smart digest triggering

- Add Powder Mountain scraper tracking James Peak terrain via Playwright
- Change terrain scrape schedule from 7am to 6am MT start
- Replace fixed digest cron with smart triggering: auto-generates after
  both avalanche forecast and terrain data are available for the day
- Keep 10am fallback digest in case auto-trigger doesn't fire
- Add Powder Mountain to weather forecasts, digest, and frontend
- Keep avalanche schedule at 5-9am as requested

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Make daily digest collapsible — starts minimized, click to expand

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix danger rose: split comma-separated string from UAC API

The UAC API returns overall_danger_rose as a comma-separated string
like "4,4,4,4,..." rather than a JSON array. The frontend was treating
each character as a data point. Now splits the string into an array
of numbers before rendering.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Add debug logging to find UAC danger rose image field

Prints all advisory keys and any fields containing image/rose/png
to help identify the correct field name for the rose image URL.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Use UAC danger rose image instead of generated SVG

Extract the rose image URL from the advisory's overall_danger_rose_image
field (HTML img tag) and display it directly. Removes the client-side
SVG rendering code, color maps, and legend CSS.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Wrap terrain scrape in try/except so avalanche fetch still runs

The scrape_all() call was crashing with a signal error in threads,
preventing fetch_avalanche_forecast() from ever executing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Debug: log all advisory keys + fix skip logic for rose image

- Print all advisory keys and values of any rose/image fields
- Only skip scheduled fetch if forecast already has danger_rose_image

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Debug: check all JSON nesting levels for rose image field

The advisory object only has danger_rose_1, not overall_danger_rose_image.
The image field is likely at a parent level. Now logs parent and top-level
keys to find it.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Fix signal.SIGALRM crash: replace with thread-safe watchdog timer

signal.signal() only works in the main thread, but run_scrape is called
from APScheduler threads and background threads. Replace with
threading.Timer watchdog that sets an Event flag, checked between steps.

Also includes debug logging for UAC API parent/top-level keys to find
the danger rose image field.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Remove debug logging from avalanche.py

Rose image field confirmed at advisory.overall_danger_rose_image.
Extraction and frontend rendering already wired up.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

* Disable auto_stop_machines and add CI/CD deploy workflow

The scheduler needs the machine to stay running to scrape on schedule.
auto_stop_machines was set to 'stop' which could halt the machine during
idle periods, preventing scheduled scrapes from running. Also adds a
GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy on push to main.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01KFfHYrgST63wqUtWws31MV

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add simplified Gradio Space deployment (2-step process)

New files:
- upload_to_hf.py: Automated script to upload FAISS indexes to HF Hub
  - Interactive prompts for HF token
  - Auto-creates dataset repository
  - Uploads hospital and corruption indexes
  - User only needs to change YOUR_HF_USERNAME

- gradio_app.py: Complete Gradio Space application
  - Auto-loads indexes from HF Hub
  - Embedding model (Qwen3) + Cross-Encoder reranker
  - Beautiful UI with configuration panel
  - Example queries included
  - API-enabled for future Vercel integration
  - Ready to copy-paste to HF Space

- DEPLOY_GUIDE.md: Step-by-step deployment guide
  - Complete 2-step process (upload + create space)
  - Detailed instructions with screenshots references
  - Troubleshooting section
  - FAQ and common issues
  - Total time: <10 minutes

Benefits:
- Reduced from 10 steps to 2 steps
- No complex configuration
- All-in-one files (just copy-paste)
- Fixed URL, permanent hosting
- 100% free (HF Space CPU)
- Future-proof (can migrate to Vercel later)

Deployment flow:
1. Run upload_to_hf.py in Colab → upload indexes
2. Create HF Space, paste gradio_app.py → done!

This replaces the complex Flask + Vercel approach while
keeping the option to migrate later (Gradio has API mode).

commit.message:
Fix numbered section rendering and follow-up suggestion format

- Numbered sections: single numbered lines (e.g., "1. Check power supply") were being
  wrapped in <ol><li> which always renders as "1." regardless of the actual number.
  Now requires 2+ consecutive list items before treating as an ordered list.
- Follow-up suggestions: changed prompt from "questions" to "short topics" (3-8 words)
  so chips show concise phrases like "OS2 wiring connections" instead of full questions.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix CRLF and SIGPIPE issues in entrypoint scripts

- Strip carriage returns in Dockerfile build step
- Replace tr|head with od-based secret generation to avoid SIGPIPE
- Add .gitattributes to enforce LF line endings for shell scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: A2A forwarding reliability & safety hardening (R1-R6)

- R1: Forwarding retry with exponential backoff (5s/15s/45s), transient
  errors retry 3x before failing, settlement mandates preserved during retry
- R2: Task timeout enforcement — sweep fails tasks stuck in working >60s
- R3: Orphan mandate cleanup — sweep cancels mandates active >1h
- R4: Usage tracking after settlement via LimitService.recordUsage()
- R5: Structured correlation logging [A2A task=xxxx tenant=yyyy] across
  task-processor, worker, and jsonrpc-handler
- R6: Per-agent circuit breaker — 5 consecutive failures opens circuit,
  60s cooldown before half-open probe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
UI overhaul: green section, nav updates, FAQ color, new footer

- HeroScrollAnimation: add id="hsa-dark" to dark section
- TubeLightNav: replace Sparkles→ShoppingCart icon, update nav items (hsa-dark, features)
- App.js: remove #how section, update IntersectionObserver target to #features, replace social footer with branded site footer
- App.css: green bg (#A8C9A4) for dark HSA section with adapted text colors; FAQ trigger color → #D9746B; remove .social-footer/.social-label/.social-handle; add .site-footer styles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add browser conditional exports to fix bundler compatibility (#41) (#63)

Browser bundlers (Vite, Rollup, webpack) fail when resolving the main
entry point because it re-exports Node-only modules (skia-canvas,
child_process). This adds a "browser" condition in package.json exports
that routes to a browser-safe entry point excluding those modules.

Changes:
- Add src/index.browser.ts and src/rendering/index.browser.ts that
  export everything except Node-only rendering functions
- Add "browser" conditional export in package.json so bundlers
  automatically resolve to the browser entry point
- Move skia-canvas to optionalDependencies
- Mark skia-canvas as external in tsup build

commit.message:
feat: complete marketplace features (Phases 2-8)

- Application management: accept/reject workers, job status transitions
- Job completion flow: worker marks done -> employer confirms -> auto-release
- Escrow payments: wallet system, hold/release/refund, 7% commission
- Internal chat: real-time messaging with anti-evasion contact filtering
- Reviews: bidirectional ratings, worker levels (Nuevo/Activo/Experto/Pro)
- Profiles: skills, bio, availability, coverage radius, KYC 3 levels
- Notifications: in-app with unread badge, auto-polling every 30s
- Admin dashboard: stats, user management, dispute resolution, KYC review
- 6 new DB migrations, 5 new services, 5 new route files
- 7 new frontend pages, 4 new components
- End-to-end tested: apply -> accept -> complete -> confirm -> review

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add logout button to admin panel

commit.message:
fix: enable cross-subdomain session persistence with httpOnly cookies

- Add withCredentials: true to axios instance and refreshTokenProactively
  so httpOnly cookies (domain=.migro.es) are sent on all cross-origin requests
- AuthProvider now attempts /users/me even without localStorage tokens,
  allowing session restore via httpOnly cookies set by the backend
- Prevent premature redirects during cookie-based auth checks on protected routes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix area pages returning 404: use rewrite for dynamic routes

Next.js App Router doesn't support partial dynamic segments in folder
names (counselling-in-[area]). Moved to /area/[slug] with a rewrite
from /counselling-in-:slug so URLs stay the same for SEO. Also added
missing Portsmouth and Southsea area page content.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
